
---
title: "QA Dashboard"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(plotly)
library(readxl)

# Load and clean data
df <- read_excel("~/Desktop/Final/Bhakti_Final/P_final/train_data.xlsx")
df <- clean_names(df)
```

Row {data-height=150}
-------------------------------------

### Total Records
```{r}
valueBox(value = nrow(df), icon = "fa-database", color = "lightblue")
```

### % High Risk
```{r}
risk_pct <- mean(df$is_high_risk == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
valueBox(value = sprintf("%.1f%%", risk_pct), icon = "fa-exclamation-triangle", color = "red")
```

### Avg Income
```{r}
avg_income <- mean(df$income, na.rm = TRUE)
valueBox(value = scales::dollar(avg_income), icon = "fa-money-bill", color = "green")
```

### Median Account Age
```{r}
median_age <- median(df$account_age, na.rm = TRUE)
valueBox(value = paste(median_age, "days"), icon = "fa-calendar", color = "purple")
```

Row {data-height=500}
-------------------------------------

### Risk by Job Title
```{r}
plot_ly(df %>% count(job_title, is_high_risk),
        x = ~n, y = ~job_title, color = ~as.factor(is_high_risk),
        type = 'bar', orientation = 'h') %>%
  layout(title = "High Risk Count by Job Title", barmode = "stack")
```



### Income Distribution by Education Level
```{r}
plot_ly(df, y = ~income, x = ~education_level,
        type = "box", color = ~education_level) %>%
  layout(title = "Income Distribution Across Education Levels",
         xaxis = list(title = "Education Level"), yaxis = list(title = "Income"))

```

Row
-------------------------------------

### Education Breakdown
```{r}
df %>% count(education_level) %>%
  plot_ly(labels = ~education_level, values = ~n, type = "pie", hole = 0.5) %>%
  layout(title = "Education Level Distribution")
```

### Heatmap: Avg Income by Education & Employment
```{r}
heat_data <- df %>%
  group_by(education_level, employment_status) %>%
  summarise(avg_income = mean(income, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

plot_ly(
  data = heat_data, x = ~employment_status, y = ~education_level,
  z = ~avg_income, type = "heatmap", colorscale = "Viridis"
) %>%
  layout(title = "Average Income by Education and Employment")
```
